title: The Movie Star Rankings: 35 Over 35, Revisited
format: Sean and Amanda rebuild the 35-over-35 movie-star ranking from scratch, arguing over Ryan Gosling's soul, Ben Affleck's ebb, and whether Vin Diesel deserves Guardians of the Galaxy credit, before landing on Tom Cruise over Denzel Washington and Leonardo DiCaprio.

segments.heading: The state of movie stardom
segments.note: the NRG survey, the ground rules, and the honorary icons

films.title: Fantastic Beasts: The Secrets of Dumbledore
films.note: Cited against a new National Research Group survey ranking Johnny Depp the No. 9 movie star in America: he was ejected from this franchise and is widely considered unemployable in Hollywood.

films.title: Black Adam
films.note: The Rock came in at No. 2 on the same survey off the back of this, which they call a significant failure.

films.title: Red Notice
films.note: The Rock's other recent credit, which "every human who's seen it can agree stinks," and which never played in theaters.

films.title: Wonka
films.note: Passing dig at how much of Timothée Chalamet's fame is TikTok/Instagram gossip about Kylie Jenner rather than movie stardom.

films.title: Book Club: The Next Chapter
films.note: Opens the same weekend as this taping, headlined by several of the show's honorary "icon status" actors like De Niro and Keaton.

films.title: The Flash
films.note: Cited as proof Michael Keaton, newly revealed to be 71, is still landing major studio roles.

films.title: Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny
films.note: Raised to question whether Harrison Ford, an icon-status honoree, can still open something outside this franchise.

segments.heading: The toughest cuts
segments.note: ten stars dropped from the last list, a run of quicker cuts, and two international picks

films.title: Ghosted
films.note: Chris Evans's cut: "You fucked up, bro."

films.title: Eileen
films.note: Anne Hathaway's Sundance premiere, cited as her likely way back onto a future list.

films.title: Roald Dahl's The Witches
films.note: Part of the rough stretch behind Hathaway's cut, alongside a WeCrashed TV run.

films.title: Black Panther: Wakanda Forever
films.note: Lupita Nyong'o's most recent credit; otherwise she "hasn't really done a whole lot."

films.title: The 355
films.note: The Nyong'o bomb cited as part of her quiet stretch.

films.title: The Woman in the Window
films.note: One of five straight Amy Adams credits read aloud as "the worst slump in some time."

films.title: Hillbilly Elegy
films.note: Another entry in the Amy Adams slump list.

films.title: Zack Snyder's Justice League
films.note: Another entry in the Amy Adams slump list.

films.title: Dear Evan Hansen
films.note: Another entry in the Amy Adams slump list.

films.title: Disenchanted
films.note: The fifth and final credit in the Amy Adams slump list.

films.title: Arrival
films.note: Cited in Adams's defense: she used to alternate stinkers with genuinely great movies.

films.title: The Card Counter
films.note: Oscar Isaac, compelling as "a monastic psycho," but not quite a movie-star turn.

films.title: Dune
films.note: Isaac's brief reign as Duke Leto, cited approvingly despite his cut.

films.title: Creed III
films.note: Tessa Thompson's most recent credit, playing a character with "a little bit more going on than the wife partner," but not enough to keep her on.

films.title: The Little Mermaid
films.note: Melissa McCarthy is about to play Ursula in it, not quite enough to save her cut.

films.title: Hypnotic
films.note: Ben Affleck's new one, from a now-defunct studio; cited as proof he's in "an ebb" as an actor but "a flow" as a director.

films.title: Emancipation
films.note: Will Smith's Apple TV+ film, not a success, part of the case for his cut after the Oscars slap.

films.title: Bad Boys: Ride or Die
films.note: Referred to as "Bad Boys 4" at the time; called a retrenchment meant to remind people what they love about Will Smith.

films.title: Father Stu
films.note: Mark Wahlberg's flop, cited as the end of a phase for him even though he was never actually on either list.

films.title: Venom: Let There Be Carnage
films.note: One of two recent Venom films that keep Tom Hardy off the list; "I need Tom Hardy to make more movies."

films.title: Capone
films.note: Tom Hardy's other recent credit: "so bad," Josh Trank's follow-up to Chronicle.

films.title: Maleficent: Mistress of Evil
films.note: Cited among Angelina Jolie's recent credits, deemed not enough to make the cut.

films.title: Those Who Wish Me Dead
films.note: Another Angelina Jolie credit weighed and found wanting.

films.title: Eternals
films.note: Angelina Jolie's MCU credit, also not enough to make the cut.

films.title: Next Goal Wins
films.note: One of two Michael Fassbender films this fall cited as a possible comeback.

films.title: The Killer
films.note: The other Fassbender film this fall; "it's been 22 years since Michael Fassbender appeared in a film," Amanda jokes.

films.title: Star Wars: Episode I - The Phantom Menace
films.note: Cited in passing during the Natalie Portman discussion, and separately as the trailer Amanda's toddler watched on repeat.

films.title: Coming 2 America
films.note: The case against Eddie Murphy's cut; his 2019 comeback in Dolemite is deemed too far back now.

films.title: You People
films.note: Eddie Murphy's other recent credit, and separately part of Jonah Hill's eligibility case.

films.title: Dolemite Is My Name
films.note: Eddie Murphy's 2019 comeback, deemed too far back to keep him on the list now.

films.title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
films.note: Edward Norton's part of a movie that "didn't open in theaters, but if it did, would have made a lot of money."

films.title: Don't Worry Darling
films.note: Chris Pine's recent "L," though the spit-gate press tour worked in his favor.

films.title: Dungeons & Dragons: Honor Among Thieves
films.note: Cited as the movie that could put Chris Pine back on a future list.

films.title: Avatar: The Way of Water
films.note: Kate Winslet's cameo, "for like four minutes," cited against her cut.

films.title: Don't Look Up
films.note: The credit that makes Jonah Hill's eligibility questionable in the first place.

films.title: Stutz
films.note: Jonah Hill's documentary, cited as evidence he "hasn't been in the frame" as an actor lately.

films.title: Venom
films.note: Brought up re: Michelle Williams, who once apologized for it on The Rewatchables; called "the classic great actor, not quite movie star" case.

films.title: The Upside
films.note: Kevin Hart's more serious-register hit, made for $40 million and grossing $130 million; cited as proof he could be a top-15 star if he chose to.

films.title: The Mother
films.note: Jennifer Lopez's new Netflix film, out the day of taping.

films.title: Shotgun Wedding
films.note: J. Lo's Amazon movie, reportedly a huge streaming hit now getting a sequel.

films.title: Marry Me
films.note: J. Lo's Peacock film with Owen Wilson, also cited as a streaming hit.

films.title: Second Act
films.note: The J. Lo movie Sean almost saw in a Palm Springs second-run theater and didn't.

films.title: Dune
films.note: Jason Momoa is "phenomenal" but "the eighth lead" in it, per Amanda; his character's fate is a running joke.

films.title: The Creator
films.note: John David Washington's big-buzz CinemaCon sci-fi tentpole, misattributed on air to "Gareth Evans" rather than director Gareth Edwards.

films.title: Infinity Pool
films.note: International pick Alexander Skarsgård, cited as a strategic move away from being "just a TV star."

films.title: Lee
films.note: Skarsgård's upcoming film with Kate Winslet and Marion Cotillard.

films.title: RRR
films.note: Amanda's international pick: N. T. Rama Rao Jr. and Ram Charan, huge stars in India newly discovered by Netflix audiences.

films.title: The Gray Man
films.note: No. 35 Ryan Gosling: the paycheck movie Amanda holds against him, a "betrayal" of the Drive/Half Nelson version of Gosling she knows.

films.title: Barbie
films.note: No. 35 Ryan Gosling: the Greta Gerwig/Noah Baumbach film that could go either way, per Sean.

films.title: The Fall Guy
films.note: No. 35 Ryan Gosling: the David Leitch action film with Emily Blunt, which Amanda says feels more like his flavor.

films.title: Nope
films.note: No. 34 Steven Yeun: cited as slipping his ranking down slightly since it had already come and gone by last list.

films.title: Mickey 17
films.note: No. 34 Steven Yeun: the new Bong Joon-ho movie he's set to star in, following Yeun's role in Okja.

films.title: Thunderbolts*
films.note: No. 34 Steven Yeun: his rumored upcoming MCU role, framed as evidence of a rare Korean American star reaching this scale.

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: No. 33 Emily Blunt: playing the wife role, part of her big year coming up.

films.title: Pain Hustlers
films.note: No. 33 Emily Blunt: a David Yates adaptation of a Wells Tower screenplay about the opioid crisis, also starring Chris Evans.

films.title: The Fall Guy
films.note: No. 33 Emily Blunt: also opposite Ryan Gosling.

films.title: Thor: Love and Thunder
films.note: No. 32 Chris Hemsworth: not very successful in their opinion, though it still opened big.

films.title: Extraction 2
films.note: No. 32 Chris Hemsworth: the trailer alone got a "put it inside me" from Amanda.

films.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
films.note: No. 32 Chris Hemsworth: the Mad Max prequel he's also set to appear in.

films.title: Swan Song
films.note: No. 31 Mahershala Ali: a soft-science-fiction Apple TV+ film that made Amanda cry, late in her pregnancy.

films.title: Leave the World Behind
films.note: No. 31 Mahershala Ali: the new Sam Esmail film he stars in, out that November.

films.title: House of Gucci
films.note: No. 30 Adam Driver: one of the four big movies he had ahead of him last time, none of which drove audiences the way expected.

films.title: White Noise
films.note: No. 30 Adam Driver: another of the four.
No. 30 Adam Driver: the last of the four, cited earlier as an example of the vagaries of the IP era.

films.title: The Lost City
films.note: No. 29 Channing Tatum: alongside Dog, the pair of 2022 films that reannounced his power as a movie star.

films.title: Dog
films.note: No. 29 Channing Tatum: made with producing partner Reid Carolin, cited as a model for the mid-budget hit.

films.title: Magic Mike's Last Dance
films.note: No. 29 Channing Tatum: "no one saw it," a rare miss in his comeback stretch.

films.title: Fast X
films.note: No. 28 Vin Diesel: the reason he's finally back on the list, a week from release at the time of taping.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
films.note: No. 28 Vin Diesel: his voice role as Groot, though voice work doesn't formally qualify.

films.title: Avatar: The Way of Water
films.note: No. 27 Zoe Saldana: called the most important character in the film even more than Jake Sully, per Amanda.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
films.note: No. 27 Zoe Saldana: as Gamora, an example of stardom "subsumed by the IP."

films.title: Amsterdam
films.note: No. 27 Zoe Saldana: playing a nurse, cited as proof she could do more outside franchise work.

films.title: Thor: Love and Thunder
films.note: No. 26 Christian Bale: not a good movie in their view, but he's great in it.

films.title: Amsterdam
films.note: No. 26 Christian Bale: a box-office disaster, though not one they blame on him.

films.title: The Pale Blue Eye
films.note: No. 26 Christian Bale: the Scott Cooper Edgar Allan Poe mystery, straight to Netflix.

films.title: The Church of Living Dangerously
films.note: No. 26 Christian Bale: an announced-but-unreleased project (Bale as a drug-smuggling preacher) that Amanda jokes she can't identify; still unproduced as of taping.

films.title: Zero Dark Thirty
films.note: No. 25 Jessica Chastain: the film she's remembered for before this list, now that she finally has an Oscar.

films.title: The Eyes of Tammy Faye
films.note: No. 25 Jessica Chastain: the Oscar-winning performance, plus makeup.

films.title: The Good Nurse
films.note: No. 25 Jessica Chastain: "I watched it to the end. That's where I stand on it."

films.title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
films.note: No. 24 Daniel Craig: one of the most-watched movies of last year, even on a streaming service.

films.title: Queer
films.note: No. 24 Daniel Craig: the Luca Guadagnino film Sean would love for him.

films.title: Air
films.note: No. 23 Matt Damon: expected to be seen by "north of 700 billion people" on Prime Video, per Sean, and quite good in it.

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: No. 23 Matt Damon: playing what's likely a supporting lead.

films.title: Stillwater
films.note: No. 23 Matt Damon: "grievously miscast," per Sean, in one of the weirdest mainstream films in memory.

films.title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
films.note: No. 22 Michelle Yeoh: the Oscar win that reminded everyone she's "one of the greats."

films.title: Crazy Rich Asians
films.note: No. 22 Michelle Yeoh: cited as the actual start of her renaissance.

films.title: A Haunting in Venice
films.note: No. 22 Michelle Yeoh: the Kenneth Branagh film Sean pre-declares "an absolute travesty."

films.title: Free Guy
films.note: No. 21 Ryan Reynolds: a huge hit that "destroyed my soul," per Amanda.

films.title: Deadpool & Wolverine
films.note: No. 21 Ryan Reynolds: referred to on air as "Deadpool 3," with Hugh Jackman set to costar.

films.title: IF
films.note: No. 21 Ryan Reynolds: the John Krasinski-directed high-concept project he's set to star in.

films.title: The Batman
films.note: No. 20 Robert Pattinson: a year old by the time of taping, cited as making his ranking feel slightly dated.

films.title: Tenet
films.note: No. 20 Robert Pattinson: the Nolan film Sean insists everyone actually liked.

films.title: Mickey 17
films.note: No. 20 Robert Pattinson: his upcoming Bong Joon-ho lead role.

films.title: The Lost City
films.note: No. 19 Sandra Bullock: her comeback, a standalone $100 million hit she opened wearing a catsuit.

films.title: Bullet Train
films.note: No. 19 Sandra Bullock: a trade-off cameo the same year.

films.title: Bird Box Barcelona
films.note: No. 19 Sandra Bullock: not actually in it, but cited as a spinoff of her Netflix hit worth watching for.

films.title: Beau Is Afraid
films.note: No. 18 Joaquin Phoenix: the most debated movie of the year so far, at a minimum.

films.title: Napoleon
films.note: No. 18 Joaquin Phoenix: one of two very high-profile films coming, playing the title role for Ridley Scott.

films.title: Joker: Folie à Deux
films.note: No. 18 Joaquin Phoenix: the musical Joker sequel with Lady Gaga.

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: No. 17 Robert Downey Jr.: his pivot back from Iron Man toward serious work.

films.title: Sr.
films.note: No. 17 Robert Downey Jr.: the documentary about his father, made with Chris Smith, that Sean loved.

films.title: Maestro
films.note: No. 16 Bradley Cooper: the Leonard Bernstein biopic he directed and stars in, wearing what Amanda calls "a lot of old person makeup."

films.title: Nightmare Alley
films.note: No. 16 Bradley Cooper: an Academy Award Best Picture nominee.

films.title: Licorice Pizza
films.note: No. 16 Bradley Cooper: his memorable Jon Peters cameo.

films.title: Asteroid City
films.note: No. 15 Scarlett Johansson: the new Wes Anderson movie, opening in about three weeks; she plays a movie star in it.

films.title: Jojo Rabbit
films.note: No. 15 Scarlett Johansson: one of the auteur projects she sprinkled in around her MCU run.

films.title: Fly Me to the Moon
films.note: No. 15 Scarlett Johansson: discussed under its working title, Project Artemis, a space-race project also starring Channing Tatum.

films.title: Hustle
films.note: No. 14 Adam Sandler: won him an Independent Spirit Award, proof that making Netflix movies doesn't disqualify him as a movie star.

films.title: Murder Mystery 2
films.note: No. 14 Adam Sandler: "quite poor, but I watched it. Literally all of it," says Amanda.

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: No. 14 Adam Sandler: "one of the last good things we had in this world before the pandemic."

films.title: Black Adam
films.note: No. 13 Dwayne Johnson: his movies "stink," per Sean, even as his charisma remains undeniable.

films.title: Red Notice
films.note: No. 13 Dwayne Johnson: another entry in a run of quality complaints against him.

films.title: TÁR
films.note: No. 12 Cate Blanchett: gave the world Lydia Tár, "the most important person forever," Oscar snub notwithstanding.

films.title: Thor: Ragnarok
films.note: No. 12 Cate Blanchett: cited as one of her more mainstream dips.

films.title: Borderlands
films.note: No. 12 Cate Blanchett: her second film in a row made with director Eli Roth, who left partway through production.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
films.note: No. 11 Chris Pratt: reason enough that "no one on this podcast can remember" the Jurassic films exist despite the money they make.

films.title: Jurassic World Dominion
films.note: No. 11 Chris Pratt: part of a franchise that keeps printing money regardless of memorability.

films.title: The Super Mario Bros. Movie
films.note: No. 11 Chris Pratt: his voice role, which "doesn't count" by their rules but made an enormous amount of money.

films.title: Leave the World Behind
films.note: No. 10 Julia Roberts: Sean's "most anticipated movie of the year," and part of what makes her barely eligible.

films.title: Ticket to Paradise
films.note: No. 10 Julia Roberts.

films.title: Ben Is Back
films.note: No. 10 Julia Roberts: the qualifying third film, and one they think she's wonderful in.

films.title: A Man Called Otto
films.note: No. 9 Tom Hanks: made $140 million and became Netflix's top streaming film; "it's not good, but it's fine."

films.title: Elvis
films.note: No. 9 Tom Hanks: made a tremendous amount of money on his and Austin Butler's names both.

films.title: Creed III
films.note: No. 8 Michael B. Jordan: a surprisingly bigger hit than Creed II.

films.title: Without Remorse
films.note: No. 8 Michael B. Jordan: not a very good Amazon film, but the basis of the Rainbow Six spinoff he's developing.

films.title: Just Mercy
films.note: No. 8 Michael B. Jordan: an example of the more prestigious work he dips into occasionally.

films.title: Fast X
films.note: No. 7 Charlize Theron: her one upcoming credit, after a fall from No. 2 on the last list.

films.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
films.note: No. 7 Charlize Theron: a surprise post-credits appearance, part of an otherwise very quiet stretch.

films.title: Babylon
films.note: No. 6 Brad Pitt: called one of the greatest films of their times.

films.title: Bullet Train
films.note: No. 6 Brad Pitt: forgettable, per Sean, but plenty of people have watched it and forgotten it.

films.title: F1
films.note: No. 6 Brad Pitt: an Apple-backed racing drama from Joseph Kosinski, framed as classic star-driven packaging.

films.title: Air
films.note: No. 5 Viola Davis: expected to earn her an Oscar nomination, per Sean, even in a historically loose performance.

films.title: The Woman King
films.note: No. 5 Viola Davis: should have been nominated, in their view.

films.title: The Hunger Games: The Ballad of Songbirds & Snakes
films.note: No. 5 Viola Davis: her next, prompting a "songbird or snake" bit.

films.title: John Wick: Chapter 4
films.note: No. 4 Keanu Reeves: called Sean's actual favorite movie of the year so far.

films.title: The Matrix Resurrections
films.note: No. 4 Keanu Reeves: "most people didn't" like it, but he did.

films.title: Ballerina
films.note: No. 4 Keanu Reeves: the John Wick-universe spinoff he's expected to appear in.

films.title: Killers of the Flower Moon
films.note: No. 3 Leonardo DiCaprio: his third film in roughly as many years, premiering the following week in France without them.

films.title: Don't Look Up
films.note: No. 3 Leonardo DiCaprio: reportedly the most-watched film in Netflix history at the time.

films.title: The Equalizer 3
films.note: No. 2 Denzel Washington: the trailer alone got a "this looks fucking sick" text to Bill Simmons and Chris Ryan.

films.title: Gladiator II
films.note: No. 2 Denzel Washington: an unconfirmed-at-the-time Ridley Scott sequel rumored to also include Paul Mescal, Barry Keoghan, and Pedro Pascal.

films.title: Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Part One
films.note: No. 1 Tom Cruise: the summer blockbuster Sean says he's never been more excited for, save for Maverick.

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: No. 1 Tom Cruise: the campaign that got the crown back, per Sean, calling it "very strange" and "very effective."
